title: Kafka Consumers Deep Dive
learningObjectives: Explain **poll** semantics and how **max.poll.interval** ties to handler runtime
Compare **auto**, **sync**, and **async** commits with failure scenarios
Use **pause**/**resume** and **rebalance** listeners for production-safe consumers
Contrast **static** membership and cooperative rebalance trade-offs

sections.title: Why Kafka Consumers Deep Dive matters
sections.content:
**Consumer** tuning is where **SLOs** meet **reality**: **poll** cadence, **commit** points, and **rebalance** behavior decide whether you ship **duplicates**, **loss**, or **lag**. Interviewers love **max.poll.interval** war stories.

This day pairs with **producer** durability: **end-to-end** thinking separates **senior** answers from **tutorial** answers.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Kafka Consumers Deep Dive
sections.content:
### Day 61 — **Kafka** **consumers**

### 1. Poll loop
**poll** balances **fetch**, **heartbeat**, and **rebalance** participation.

### 2. Offset commit modes
**Auto** vs **sync** manual vs **async** manual—each trades **latency**, **throughput**, and **failure** semantics.

### 3. Rebalance
**Partition** **revocation** **stops** **processing**—design **idempotent** **handlers**.

### 4. Backpressure
**pause**/**resume** and **downstream** **queue** depths.

### 5. Isolation level
Interacts with **transactional** **producers**.

### 6. Story
**Auto** commit + DB write reorder → **finance** mismatch; **manual** commit after **persist**.

sections.title: Basic — Consumer vocabulary
sections.output:
poll: fetch + heartbeat driver
commit: persist group offset cursor
rebalance: partition ownership moves

sections.title: Intermediate — Commit after process simulation
sections.description: Toy state machine: processed flag gates commit.

sections.title: Advanced — Per-partition lag toy
Consumer group coordinator

@startuml
title Day 61 — Consumer group
participant Coordinator
participant Consumer
Coordinator -> Consumer : assignment
loop poll
  Consumer -> Coordinator : heartbeat + fetch
end
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Kafka consumers — offset commit modes (Day 61 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly** the assignment line on offset commit trade-offs.
hints: One println, match assignments JSON verbatim.
Name one **metric** and one **config** you would check on-call for this day’s topic.
State a **failure mode** (e.g. rebalance, ISR shrink, schema break) tied to your answer.

sections.title: Use cases — Kafka Consumers Deep Dive
sections.content:
## Use cases — **Kafka consumers**

### 1) **Fraud** **scoring** **pipeline**
**Context:** **Sub-second** **latency** with **CPU**-heavy **models** per message.
**Why Day 61:** **max.poll.interval.ms** vs **handler** runtime causes **rebalance** storms.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Offload** work to **workers**; keep **poll** loop **non-blocking**.
2. Tune **max.poll.records** with **processing** SLA.
3. Use **pause**/**resume** when **downstream** is **unhealthy**.
**Failure hook:** **Lag** **jumps** after **model** deploy → **poll** **timeout**.

### 2) **Billing** **usage** **aggregation**
**Context:** **At-least-once** delivery; **duplicates** must not **double**-count.
**Why Day 61:** **Offset** **commit** order vs **side** **effects**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Idempotent** **upsert** keyed by **event** **id**.
2. **Commit** **after** **successful** **write** (or use **transactions** where justified).
3. Track **consumer** **lag** per **partition** for **tenant** fairness.
**Failure hook:** **Rebalance** → **reprocess** **window** without **idempotency**.

### 3) **CDC** **fan-out** **consumer**
**Context:** **High** **throughput** **changelog** from **database** **capture**.
**Why Day 61:** **fetch** tuning and **partition** **assignment** stability matter.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Prefer **cooperative** rebalance to reduce **stop-the-world**.
2. Size **threads** to **partition** count; avoid **idle** **consumers**.
3. **Dead-letter** **tombstone** or **schema** errors.
**Failure hook:** **Single** **poison** **partition** blocks **ordering** for a **shard**.

### 4) **Multi-region** **drill**
**Context:** **Failover** **consumers** to **secondary** cluster.
**Why Day 61:** **Group** **generation**, **offsets**, and **mirror** lag must be **rehearsed**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Document **offset** **mapping** or **timestamp** **reset** policy.
2. Run **dry-run** **failover** quarterly with **lag** **SLO** checks.
**Failure hook:** **Dual** **active** **consumers** after **DNS** **flap**.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is **poll** loop responsibility?
conceptual.answer: **poll** **fetches** records, **drives** **partition** **fetch** **negotiation**, and **sends** **heartbeats**; **long** gaps **trigger** **rebalance**. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is **poll** loop responsibility?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **enable.auto.commit** behavior?
conceptual.answer: Consumer **periodically** **commits** **offsets** **without** your **code** **choosing** **exactly** **after** **success** **processing**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****enable.auto.commit** behavior?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

conceptual.question: **auto.offset.reset** latest vs earliest?
conceptual.answer: Where to **start** **without** **committed** **offset**; **latest** may **skip** **during** **outage** **if** **new** **group**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

conceptual.answer: **Sticky** **assignment** **without** **surprise** **rebalance** on **scale** **events** under **controlled** **ops**. conceptual.answer: **Long** **poll** **wait** for **batching** at **broker**. conceptual.answer: **Range**, **round-robin**, **sticky**, **cooperative-sticky** depending on **client** **version**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

conceptual.question: **Exactly-once** consumer myth?
conceptual.answer: **Kafka** does **not** magically **EOS** **your** **DB** **writes**; **idempotent** **application** **layer** required. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. conceptual.question: **Consumer** **interceptors** use?
conceptual.answer: **Observability** **hooks** **around** **poll** **and** **commit**—**avoid** **heavy** **work** **there**. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

conceptual.question: Why might **idempotent producer** still see duplicates downstream?
conceptual.answer: Broker-side idempotency covers **producer session** retries on the **write path**, not your **consumer** processing or **at-least-once** delivery to external systems. **End-to-end** uniqueness needs **idempotent sinks**, **keys**, or **transactions** where appropriate.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

conceptual.question: What is the practical difference between **log compaction** and **time retention**?
conceptual.answer: **Retention** drops old segments by **time/size**. **Compaction** keeps the **latest** record per key for **changelog**-style topics. Picking the wrong mode breaks **replay**, **GDPR** expectations, or **storage** forecasts.

Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// blocks until broker acks commit** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.answer: // position current fetch; committed last group commit

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// position current fetch; committed last group commit** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.question: Why might `session.timeout.ms` and `heartbeat.interval.ms` be tuned together?
codeBased.answer: **Heartbeats** must arrive **often enough** to keep the session alive while tolerating **GC** pauses and **network** jitter. **Tight** sessions cause **false** **failures** and **rebalances**; **loose** sessions slow **failure** detection.

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie ****Heartbeats** must arrive **often enough** to keep the session alive while tolerating **GC** pauses** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.question: What does Spring Kafka `@KafkaListener` **concurrency** actually scale?
codeBased.answer: It scales **threads** in the **listener container**, but **throughput** per partition is still **serialized**—you need **enough partitions** to use extra threads. Misunderstanding this leads to **idle** threads and **unchanged** lag.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Cooperative** rebalance loop after **GC** pause.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Heap dump; **pause** **the** **world** metrics.

**(2) Root cause** **max.poll.interval** exceeded.

**(3) Fix** Tune **GC** or **raise** interval carefully; **faster** handler.

**(4) Prevention** Alert **rebalance** rate. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Incident:** **Cooperative** rebalance loop after **GC** pause.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Bug:** **Auto** commit before DB **txn** **commits**.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Orders **lost** on crash.

**(2) Root cause** Offset **ahead** of **ledger**.

**(3) Fix** Manual commit after **DB** success or **outbox**.

**(4) Prevention** Integration test **kill** -9. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Bug:** **Auto** commit before DB **txn** **commits**.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Latency skew.

**(2) Root cause** Sequential **processing** **too** **slow**.

**(3) Fix** **Delegate** per partition or **add** **instances** to **match** **partition** **count**.

**(4) Prevention** Profile **poll** **loop**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. wrongAnswers: **Auto** commit is always safe — **Correction:** Can **lose** or **duplicate** relative to **processing**.
**Consumer** can **read** **uncommitted** DB and be fine — **Correction:** **Split** **brain** vs **offset**.
**Seek** fixes **broker** lag — **Correction:** **Seek** only **client** cursor; fix **throughput** separately.

sections.title: MCQ Quiz — Kafka Consumers Deep Dive
sections.description: Thirty questions from basic to advanced — Kafka Consumers Deep Dive. Read every option; distractors are plausible but wrong for a precise reason.

questions.question: [Kafka Consumers] Which statement best describes **poll loop** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **poll loop** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Consumers**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Consumers] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **max.poll.interval.ms**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **max.poll.interval.ms** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Kafka Consumers] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **session.timeout.ms**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **session.timeout.ms** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.explanation: **heartbeat.interval.ms** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Consumers**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Consumers] You see misconfiguration around **auto offset commit** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **auto offset commit** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Kafka Consumers] Which statement best describes **max.poll.interval.ms** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **max.poll.interval.ms** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Consumers**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Consumers] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **session.timeout.ms**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **session.timeout.ms** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Kafka Consumers] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **heartbeat.interval.ms**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **heartbeat.interval.ms** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Kafka Consumers] Which statement best describes **auto offset commit** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **auto offset commit** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Consumers**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Consumers] You see misconfiguration around **manual commit** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **manual commit** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.

options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **partition revocation** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

sections.title: Kafka Consumers Deep Dive — cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Rule of thumb | Interview one-liner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| poll | Fetch + heartbeat | Fetch + heartbeat |
| auto.commit | Easy but fuzzy commit point | Easy but fuzzy commit point |
| commitSync | Stronger control, blocks | Stronger control, blocks |
| rebalance | Partition moves | Partition moves |
| max.poll.interval | Must exceed handler | Must exceed handler |
| lag | highWater - position | highWater - position |
| pause | Client backpressure | Client backpressure |
| isolation | read_committed with tx | read_committed with tx |
| static membership | Fewer surprise rebalances | Fewer surprise rebalances |
| deserializer errors | DLQ / skip policy | DLQ / skip policy |
| Operational metric | Watch lag + ISR + request latency | Say lag per partition and under-replicated partitions |
| Failure drill | Broker bounce + consumer crash chaos | Prove rebalance and recovery SLOs in staging |
| Capacity | Partitions bound parallelism per group | More consumers than partitions stay idle |
| Security | ACLs least privilege per principal | Separate prod cluster or strict topic governance |
| Cost | Retention drives disk and replay cost | Tiered storage or export before cutting retention |
